headline: Walmart Expands Access to GLP-1 Support Services
summary: “You can never be too rich or too thin,” according to a famous saying, and after the rise of pricey GLP-1 medicines it seemed the two went hand in hand.  The company’s new digital health platform,  Walmart  Better Care Services, “now includes weight management support services for customers on or exploring GLP-1 therapies,” Walmart said in a statement.

headline: 3 Dividend Aristocrat ETFs to Buy Before 2026 Markets Shift
summary: Dividend growth investing rewards patience like few other strategies. The income stream compounds year after year, and the companies behind those dividends tend to be high-quality and structurally durable. With the Fed Funds rate at 3.75% and the 10-year Treasury yielding around 4.30%, dividend-focused equity funds must now compete against risk-free alternatives that offer meaningful ... 3 Dividend Aristocrat ETFs to Buy Before 2026 Markets Shift

headline: Walmart Deepens Commitment to Massachusetts, Fueling Long-Term Impact and Future Statewide Investments
summary: BOSTON, April 16, 2026--Walmart announced continued reinvestment across Massachusetts, with plans in 2026 to remodel 13 stores as part of ongoing efforts to modernize the in-store and digital shopping experience—upgrading layouts, technology, and services to offer faster, more convenient shopping and delivery in as little as an hour for most customers. These efforts reflect Walmart’s commitment to supporting economic growth and strengthening communities statewide.

headline: Walmart is Investing in Stores to Drive Speed, Convenience and Growth

headline: PepsiCo's sales jump after it cuts prices
summary: PepsiCo's decision to cut prices and some new snacks boosted demand in the first quarter.  Revenue jumped 8.5% to $19.44 billion in the January-March period compared to the same period a year ago, the Purchase, New York, company said Thursday.  PepsiCo began cutting prices on value brands like Chester's and Santitas last spring to win back customers exasperated by years of price hikes.

raw.headline: PepsiCo's sales jump after it cuts prices
raw.summary: PepsiCo's decision to cut prices and some new snacks boosted demand in the first quarter.  Revenue jumped 8.5% to $19.44 billion in the January-March period compared to the same period a year ago, the Purchase, New York, company said Thursday.  PepsiCo began cutting prices on value brands like Chester's and Santitas last spring to win back customers exasperated by years of price hikes.
